revision.comment: Adding [[Wikipedia:Short description|short description]]: "Japanese architectural stye"
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Setchuyo}} [[Category:Japanese Buddhist architecture]] [[Category:Japanese architectural history]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] to [[Category:mm]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''

{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''

{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}

{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>

{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}

It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese Buddhist architecture]] [[Category:Japanese architectural history]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:refreshing the bump]] [[Category:misc bump Category]] [[Category:Setchūyō]] [[Category:Pafges to be red]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Setchūyō]] [[sn:Setchūyō]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Setchuyo}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[architectural style]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[es:Setchūyō]] [[fr:Setchūyō]] [[ja:折衷様]] [[pt:Setchūyō]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto architecture]] born in Japan during the [[Muromachi period]] from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''[[wayō]]'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''[[zenshūyō]]''. It is exemplified by the [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[:en:Kakurin-ji|Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[Wayō]]'' *''[[Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} ]]

}}

It is exemplified by the [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[:en:Kakurin-ji|Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[:en:Shin-wayō|Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>

It is exemplified by the [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[:en:Kakurin-ji|Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[:en:Shin-wayō|Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[:en:Wayō|Wayō]]'' *''[[:en:Zenshūyō|Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese Buddhist architecture]] [[Category:Japanese architectural history]]
}}

It is exemplified by the [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]] at [[:en:Kakurin-ji|Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[:en:Shin-wayō|Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''[[:en:Wayō|Wayō]]'' *''[[:en:Zenshūyō|Zenshūyō]]''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Setchūyō]][[es:Setchūyō]][[fr:Setchūyō]][[ja:折衷様]][[pt:Setchūyō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Setchūyō]][[es:Setchūyō]][[fr:Setchūyō]][[ja:折衷様]][[pt:Setchūyō]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Setchūyō]][[es:Setchūyō]][[fr:Setchūyō]][[ja:折衷様]][[pt:Setchūyō]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q3480647]] Wikidata name: Setchūyō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Japanese architectural stye}} {{italic title}} [[File:Kakogawa Kakurinji12n4592.jpg|thumb|275 px|Kakurin-ji's Main Hall]] {{nihongo|'''''Setchūyō'''''|折衷様|lit. eclectic style}} is an {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture}} born in Japan during the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} from the fusion of elements from three different antecedent styles: ''{{ill|Wayō|en|Wayō|WD=Q3566879|lt=Wayō}}'', ''[[daibutsuyō]]'', and ''{{ill|Zenshūyō|en|Zenshūyō|WD=Q3575224|lt=Zenshūyō}}''. It is exemplified by the {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=main hall}} at [[:en:Kakurin-ji|Kakurin-ji]].<ref name="young-p44">{{Harvnb|Young|Young|2007}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&pg=PA44 p=44]</ref><ref name="fletcher-p738"/> The combination of ''wayō'' and ''daibutsuyō'' in particular became so frequent that sometimes it is classed separately by scholars under the name {{nihongo|''[[:en:Shin-wayō|Shin-wayō]]''|新和様|new ''wayō''}}.<ref name="fletcher-p738">{{Harvnb|Fletcher|Cruickshank|1996}}, [https://books.google.com/books?id=Gt1jTpXAThwC&pg=PA738 p=738]</ref>
==See also== *[[Japanese_Buddhist_architecture#Heian_period|Japanese Buddhist architecture - Heian period]] *''[[Daibutsuyō]]'' *''{{ill|Wayō|en|Wayō|WD=Q3566879|lt=Wayō}}'' *''{{ill|Zenshūyō|en|Zenshūyō|WD=Q3575224|lt=Zenshūyō}}''
==Notes== <references/>
==Bibliography== *{{cite book |last1= Fletcher |first1= Banister |author-link=Banister Fletcher |last2=Cruickshank |first2=Dan |title= [[Sir Banister Fletcher's a History of Architecture]] |url= |year=1996 |orig-year=1896 |edition=20th illustrated |publisher=Architectural Press |isbn=0-7506-2267-9 }} *{{cite book |last1= Young |first1= David |last2=Young |first2=Michiko |title= The art of Japanese architecture |series=Architecture and Interior Design |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Ag5WpEKifGEC&q=shinto+architecture |access-date=2009-11-11 |year=2007 |orig-year=2004 |edition=illustrated, revised |publisher=Tuttle Publishing |isbn=978-0-8048-3838-2 }} {{Japanese architectural elements}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}}
